results.query: free API launch OR released OR announcing developer
results.result:
### Summary of X Search for Free API Launches/Releases/Announcements (Since Jan 1, 2026)

Searches via keyword ("free API" with launch/released/announcing terms) and semantic ("new free API launches for developers") in **Latest** mode surfaced ~15 relevant posts. Focus was on genuinely free/open-source tools, new integrations, or developer-friendly announcements. No major enterprise-only or paid spam. Activity peaks around lists of public APIs, OSS Postman alternatives, and big-tech free AI model access. Low-to-moderate engagement overall (highest ~600 likes), with posts from Jan 3–Feb 4, 2026.

#### Key Relevant Posts & Launches
- **Curated Free API Lists**: Multiple shares of timeless/public repos like [public-apis](https://github.com/marcelscruz/public-apis) (1000+ no-auth REST/GraphQL APIs) and a viral list of 12+ free ones (e.g., OpenWeatherMap, REST Countries, NASA, JokeAPI, Giphy). Great for prototyping—high bookmarks/views.[[1]](https://x.com/i/status/2007450306297163800)[[2]](https://x.com/i/status/2019081792645370145)
- **OSS API Testers (Postman Alternatives)**: 
  - Hoppscotch (@hoppscotch_io): Lightweight, browser-based API tool—praised as "insane" for devs.[[3]](https://x.com/i/status/2017843384631820456)
  - Dev-tools (Go-based): Free local Postman clone; records browser requests, auto-generates tests. Newly highlighted OSS project.[[4]](https://x.com/i/status/2009534376653271106)
- **Big-Tech Free AI/Developer APIs**:
  - NVIDIA: Free API keys (no CC) for open-weight models like Kimi K2.5/GLM-4.7 at build.nvidia.com—ideal for startups.[[5]](https://x.com/i/status/2017721695889477893)
  - Google: "Vibe coding" platform launched—build/deploy AI web apps instantly, zero fees/paywalls.[[6]](https://x.com/i/status/2010633026615849445)
  - Gemini Live API in OSS language app (20+ langs, one-click deploy).[[7]](https://x.com/i/status/2015265204570030380)
- **Other Notables**: Mintlify OSS perks (free API/doc generation, Slack/Discord bots).[[8]](https://x.com/i/status/2016912165723369474) Note: Mistral Devstral 2 shifted to paid (free experiment tier remains).[[9]](https://x.com/i/status/2014714745069437155)

#### Notable Users & Accounts
- **@r0ktech** (𝐑.𝐎.𝐊 👑): Full-stack dev sharing practical free API lists—top engagement (619 likes, 48k views).[[1]](https://x.com/i/status/2007450306297163800)
- **@golangch** (Golang News): Curates Go libs/tools like dev-tools API tester (424 likes).[[4]](https://x.com/i/status/2009534376653271106)
- **@srsoumyax11** (Soumya Ranjan Sahoo): Cybersecurity focus but spot-on OSS promo (Hoppscotch).[[3]](https://x.com/i/status/2017843384631820456)
- **@thisguyknowsai** (Brady Long): AI tools, covered Google vibe platform (362 likes).[[6]](https://x.com/i/status/2010633026615849445)

#### Trends
- **OSS/No-Code Boom**: Devs hyping free Postman killers and auto-doc tools—fits productivity/automation needs (e.g., photographers/social growth via easy APIs).[[8]](https://x.com/i/status/2016912165723369474)[[3]](https://x.com/i/status/2017843384631820456)[[4]](https://x.com/i/status/2009534376653271106)
- **AI Model Free Tiers**: NVIDIA/Google pushing accessible APIs for personal AI/financial apps—open-weight focus, no crypto spam.
- **Evergreen Lists**: Free public API directories dominate searches; useful for quick integrations (e.g., lead gen via NewsAPI).[[1]](https://x.com/i/status/2007450306297163800)[[2]](https://x.com/i/status/2019081792645370145)
- Sparse "brand new" launches (mostly promotions/shares); watch @golangch/@r0ktech for updates. Older xAI mentions (free credits ended 2024) filtered out.[[10]](https://x.com/i/status/2007361387312431158)

These align with needs like personal AI assistants (NVIDIA/Google), productivity (API testers), and social growth (free media APIs). For deeper dives, check linked GitHubs/repos!

results.query: open source tool launch productivity automation
results.result:
### Summary of X Search on Open Source Productivity & Automation Tool Launches

Recent posts on X highlight a surge in **open-source tools for AI-driven automation, workflow builders, and self-hosted productivity apps**, especially since late 2025. Key trends include:
- **AI agents for enterprise/task automation**: Tools like IBM's CUGA (code-writing agent for boring tasks, integrable with LLMs) and Vercel's visual workflow builder (text-to-workflow with integrations like Slack/Linear) are gaining traction for no-code/low-code productivity.[[1]](https://x.com/i/status/2000599316121924052)[[2]](https://x.com/i/status/1993743965842231579)
- **Self-hosted alternatives to n8n/Zapier**: Launches like PyRunner (Python script scheduler, "no limits" automation) and Opal (English-prompt workflows for content/data tasks, fully free).[[3]](https://x.com/i/status/2015804707189506266)[[4]](https://x.com/i/status/2018979155107336283)
- **Broader productivity stacks**: Free office suites (LibreOffice, Apache OpenOffice) and dev tools (lazygit, Coolify self-hosting).[[5]](https://x.com/i/status/2009541491715101145)[[6]](https://x.com/i/status/2000907082032398735)
- Emphasis on **free/open-source for OSS projects**, with guides to top workflow tools and agent frameworks (e.g., Nvidia NeMo, LangChain Agent Builder).[[7]](https://x.com/i/status/2009219293322657978)[[8]](https://x.com/i/status/2008469128097497502)[[9]](https://x.com/i/status/1996265192213365080)

Engagement is moderate (likes 100-500+ on popular ones), with focus on practical, integrable tools avoiding vendor lock-in. Crypto-related treasury automations appeared but were less relevant to general productivity.

#### Notable Posts & Launches
- **IBM CUGA** (Dec 2025): Open-source agent automates enterprise tasks via code execution; demo shows file analysis/email drafting. Highly shared (261 likes). Link in post.[[1]](https://x.com/i/status/2000599316121924052)
- **PyRunner** (Jan 2026): Self-hosted Python automation runner ditching block-based tools like n8n. Beta imminent, video demo.[[3]](https://x.com/i/status/2015804707189506266)
- **Vercel Workflow Builder** (Nov 2025): OSS visual agent tool outputs code; AI-powered with templates. 241 likes, strong dev interest.[[2]](https://x.com/i/status/1993743965842231579)
- **TechLatest's Top 15 OSS Workflow Tools** (Jan 2026): Curated list for devs/AI engineers (no lock-in).[[7]](https://x.com/i/status/2009219293322657978)
- **NotebookLM MCP** (Dec 2025): Open-source "Second Brain" automation with 31 tools, HTTP-based (no Selenium).[[10]](https://x.com/i/status/2006415404302753825)

#### Key Users & Accounts
- **@DataChaz** (Clawdbot tinkerer, ex-Streamlit): Shares AI agent tools, OSS focus.[[2]](https://x.com/i/status/1993743965842231579)
- **@mervenoyann** (Hugging Face): Highlights enterprise OSS like CUGA.[[1]](https://x.com/i/status/2000599316121924052)
- **@hasan_ab_hasan** (Builder, 1M+ YT subs): Launching PyRunner, active in automation projects.[[3]](https://x.com/i/status/2015804707189506266)
- **@LangChain**: Promoting agent builders for real-world productivity (e.g., GitHub/Linear automation).[[9]](https://x.com/i/status/1996265192213365080)
- Others: @catalinmpit (Documenso), @TechlatestNet (guides).

Overall, X chatter points to 2026 as a year for **agentic, self-hosted OSS automation** boosting personal/team productivity—ideal for AI assistants, workflows, and dev tools. Check GitHub links in posts for hands-on trials.

### Summary of X Search: "New Free Tier API Developer"

Searches on X reveal a strong trend among developers sharing **lists of free or free-tier APIs** for quick prototyping, side projects, and AI experimentation. Popular categories include weather, news, open data, sports, and especially **AI/ML models** (e.g., OpenAI, Gemini, HuggingFace with generous free tiers).[[1]](https://x.com/i/status/1991737349802455054) Community-curated GitHub repos like one with **1400+ completely free APIs** (weather, crypto, music, etc.) are frequently reposted and praised for no-auth access.[[2]](https://x.com/i/status/1983207491962892664) Another repo, public-apis, lists 1000+ open APIs categorized for devs.[[3]](https://x.com/i/status/2019081792645370145)

#### Recent "New" Free Tier Announcements (2025-2026):
- **Autodesk Platform Services (APS)** launched a **new Free Tier and Developer Hub** for API experimentation and access management, highlighted by engineering firm AtkinRéalis.[[4]](https://x.com/i/status/2013179900648120438)
- **TapTools** made their **blockchain data API completely free**—no cost or barriers for devs.[[5]](https://x.com/i/status/1884361052382715953)
- **NVIDIA** offers **free API keys** (no credit card) for open-weight models like Kimi K2.5 and GLM-4.7 via build.nvidia.com—ideal for startups.[[6]](https://x.com/i/status/2017721695889477893)
- Speculation around **Google Gemini 2.5 Pro "freebie" variant** appearing in the developer API, hinting at potential new free access.[[7]](https://x.com/i/status/1930669109630349677)
- **Mistral's Devstral 2** shifted to paid API but retains **free usage** under the Studio Experiment plan.[[8]](https://x.com/i/status/2014714745069437155)

#### Notable Users & Trends:
- **@sahnlam** (ByteByteGo co-founder): Viral threads on useful free-tier APIs across categories; high engagement (1k+ likes).[[1]](https://x.com/i/status/1991737349802455054)
- **@unwind_ai_**: Promotes massive free API repo; appeals to AI builders (92k+ GitHub stars).[[2]](https://x.com/i/status/1983207491962892664)
- **@AutodeskAPS** & **@TapTools**: Official announcements of new free tiers.[[4]](https://x.com/i/status/2013179900648120438)[[5]](https://x.com/i/status/1884361052382715953)
- **Trend**: Posts spike around AI APIs (e.g., free tiers from Anthropic/OpenAI alternatives like DeepSeek/Kimi).[[9]](https://x.com/i/status/2017171009753993306) Devs emphasize "zero-cost" for OSS/side projects, with lists recirculating (e.g., OpenWeather, NASA, NewsAPI).[[10]](https://x.com/i/status/1933471603070796066)[[11]](https://x.com/i/status/2007450306297163800) No major crypto/NFT spam; focus on practical, open-source tools. Activity peaks in late 2025–early 2026, with Latest mode showing niche updates like Mintlify's OSS free program.[[12]](https://x.com/i/status/2016912165723369474)

Overall, X is a goldmine for **curated free API directories** rather than daily "new launches," but watch @AutodeskAPS-types for fresh tiers. For more, check public-apis.org or the 1400+ repo! [[3]](https://x.com/i/status/2019081792645370145)

results.query: AI agent tool free OR open source
results.result:
### Overview of Findings
Recent posts on X (as of early February 2026) show strong interest in **open-source AI agent frameworks**, particularly self-hosted tools for personal automation, productivity, and security scanning. Discussions emphasize local deployment for privacy, zero-code builders, and integrations with LLMs like Claude. Popularity spikes around viral projects like OpenClaw, with trends toward agentic workflows (e.g., task automation via messaging apps) but warnings on security risks like prompt injection.[[1]](https://x.com/i/status/2019125193093050402)[[2]](https://x.com/i/status/2016161002333855808)

Key trends:
- **Self-hosted/local agents**: Shift from cloud to on-device for control (e.g., WhatsApp/Telegram integration).
- **Zero-code/no-code**: Tools like AutoAgent allow natural language agent creation.
- **Security focus**: Tools auditing AI agents themselves (e.g., MEDUSA).
- **Dev-friendly frameworks**: Python/TS/Unity for custom builds; workshops/tutorials proliferating.
- Crypto spam (e.g., $GOLEM, $Dave tokens) pollutes searches but ignored here as non-tools.[[3]](https://x.com/i/status/2018806727844766163)

### Highlighted Free/Open-Source Tools
1. **OpenClaw** (formerly Clawdbot/Moltbot): Most discussed (~160k GitHub stars). Lightweight, self-hosted framework for proactive personal AI agents. Handles emails, calendars, bookings, web browsing, code execution via messaging apps (WhatsApp, Telegram, Slack, etc.). Runs on Mac/Windows/Linux/VPS. Users praise automation (e.g., restaurant bookings via voice gen) but note vulnerabilities (RCE CVE-2026-25253, credential leaks). Ideal for personal AI assistants.[[4]](https://x.com/i/status/2019303156505801055)[[5]](https://x.com/i/status/2019200361572233712)[[6]](https://x.com/i/status/2019098005182509404)[[7]](https://x.com/i/status/2018976024143519821)[[8]](https://x.com/i/status/2018966967026344214)[[9]](https://x.com/i/status/2017905368345530387)
   - GitHub: Likely openclaw.ai or forks.
   - Users: @uxuycom, @EmGaiNhaQue, @CryptoBlckParty (security analysis).

2. **AutoAgent**: Zero-code framework to build/deploy multi-agent teams or RAG apps from prompts. Fully automated, natural language-driven. Great for productivity without coding.[[10]](https://x.com/i/status/1900960958601699792)[[11]](https://x.com/i/status/1892930853094703616)[[12]](https://x.com/i/status/1930150039281905981)
   - Users: @unwind_ai_, @Sumanth_077.

3. **MEDUSA**: Open-source CLI SAST scanner with 74 scanners + 180+ AI agent rules (OWASP LLM Top 10). Covers 42+ langs (Python, JS, Go, etc.). Targets false positives in agentic AI dev.[[2]](https://x.com/i/status/2016161002333855808)
   - User: @The_Cyber_News (659 likes).

4. **Obelisk-core**: New Python AI agent framework from @theobeliskai experiment.[[13]](https://x.com/i/status/2016605251088924893)
   - GitHub: github.com/ohnodev/obelisk-core.
   - User: @TheBasedCabal.

5. **Other notables**:
   - **GOLEM**: Open-source Unity framework; Claude AI controls 3D characters (photographer/social growth potential).[[3]](https://x.com/i/status/2018806727844766163)
   - **Agent Lightning**: Microsoft-backed? Fast/modular IDE integration (VSCode/Vim).[[14]](https://x.com/i/status/2017250328446652666)
   - **Mastra.ai**: TypeScript for web devs (Next.js agents).[[15]](https://x.com/i/status/2018556931863163020)
   - **Motia/AutoAgent variants**: Multi-lang multi-agent builders.[[16]](https://x.com/i/status/1902547400893591584)

### Key Users & Communities
- Builders/Educators: @freeCodeCamp (Claude agent tutorial, 590 likes),[[17]](https://x.com/i/status/2015711596413124683) @Al_Grigor (Snowflake/TruLens workshop), @LiorOnAI (Agents lib).
- Security: @The_Cyber_News, @CryptoBlckParty.
- Devs: @icantcodefyi (TS agents), @kwonlabs (Claude plugins).

Engagement is high on tutorials (e.g., build-your-own agents) and warnings. For productivity/financial advisors: OpenClaw/AutoAgent excel in task automation; photographers could use GOLEM for social avatars. Check GitHubs for latest—many are rapidly evolving.[[18]](https://x.com/i/status/2018607753313620055)

### Summary of X Search on "Automation Workflow Free Tool"

Recent discussions on X highlight a surge in **open-source and free-tier tools** for building no-code/low-code workflow automations, often as alternatives to paid services like Zapier. Focus is on AI-integrated platforms for agents, data pipelines, content creation, and business ops. Posts from late 2025 to early 2026 emphasize self-hosted, customizable solutions amid rising AI agent hype.[[1]](https://x.com/i/status/1993078724347281881)[[2]](https://x.com/i/status/1985874969281142809)

#### Key Tools & Projects Mentioned
- **n8n**: Dominant open-source workflow automation tool with 200+ integrations. Praised for beginner-friendly guides and as a "Zapier killer" for node-based logic via English prompts. FreeCodeCamp shared a full tutorial.[[2]](https://x.com/i/status/1985874969281142809)[[3]](https://x.com/i/status/2018979155107336283)
- **Workflow Builder**: New open-source template (Nov 2025 launch) for agent/data workflows, powered by WorkflowDevKit. Demo live at workflow-builder.dev.[[1]](https://x.com/i/status/1993078724347281881)
- **Dify**: Free visual LLM workflow builder with "long-term memory" for AI agents and business automation.[[4]](https://x.com/i/status/2018542022534308135)
- **Make.com** (free tier): Automates content workflows (e.g., blog → LinkedIn/tweets). Users report 2-hour tasks down to 10 seconds.[[5]](https://x.com/i/status/2016389445231014072)
- Others: Opal (prompt-based n8n alternative), NotebookLM (Google's free SEO/content automation).[[3]](https://x.com/i/status/2018979155107336283)[[6]](https://x.com/i/status/2015933101659324475)

#### Notable Users & Posts
- **@ctatedev (Chris Tate)**: Launched Workflow Builder; high engagement (672 likes).[[1]](https://x.com/i/status/1993078724347281881)
- **@freeCodeCamp**: Educational post on n8n (839 likes, 125 reposts).[[2]](https://x.com/i/status/1985874969281142809)
- **@tom_doerr**: Frequent sharer of OSS tools like n8n alternatives (1k+ likes on one post).[[7]](https://x.com/i/status/1893465230333600061)
- **@NetworkChuck**: YouTube guide on ditching paid tools (361 likes).[[8]](https://x.com/i/status/1972028855045988402)
- **@Spidey666246 & @SimonHoiberg**: Recent shoutouts to Dify/n8n for ops.[[4]](https://x.com/i/status/2018542022534308135)[[9]](https://x.com/i/status/2017586435226734654)

#### Trends
- **AI-Agent Shift**: Tools blending workflows with LLMs/agents (e.g., Dify, Workflow Builder) for "passive income" automations like content gen.[[1]](https://x.com/i/status/1993078724347281881)[[4]](https://x.com/i/status/2018542022534308135)
- **Open-Source Boom**: Push for self-hosted (n8n, Dify) to avoid vendor lock-in; posts from devs/builders dominate.[[7]](https://x.com/i/status/1893465230333600061)[[10]](https://x.com/i/status/1881437158231224695)
- **Free-Tier Hacks**: Tutorials for ROI calcs, SEO/content pipelines using Google tools (NotebookLM) or Make.com.[[11]](https://x.com/i/status/2017940084914278791)[[6]](https://x.com/i/status/2015933101659324475)
- Niche: Productivity for creators/recruiters (e.g., @cogitoautomate's CandidateAI).[[12]](https://x.com/i/status/2016949972441112585)

Overall, momentum around **n8n & Dify** for productivity/automation; ideal for personal AI assistants or photographers scaling social workflows. Check @tom_doerr & @freeCodeCamp for more OSS gems.[[2]](https://x.com/i/status/1985874969281142809)[[7]](https://x.com/i/status/1893465230333600061)
